name: libparse-win32registry-perl
title: libparse-win32registry-perl
summary: Perl module for parsing Windows registry files
description:
Perl module for parsing Windows registry files
Parse::Win32Registry is a module for parsing Windows Registry files, allowing
you to read the keys and values of a registry file without going through the
Windows API.
.
It provides an object-oriented interface to the keys and values in a registry
file. Registry files are structured as trees of keys, with each key
containing further subkeys or values.
.
It supports both Windows NT registry files (Windows NT, 2000, XP, 2003,
Vista) and Windows 95 registry files (Windows 95, 98, and Millennium
Edition).
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use libparse-win32registry-perl because Perl module for parsing Windows registry files.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use libparse-win32registry-perl as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair libparse-win32registry-perl with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `libparse-win32registry-perl --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
